question: What role does NVLink play in the GB200 NVL72 architecture, and how does it differ from PCIe-based GPU communication?
findings: The GB200 NVL72 uses fifth-generation NVLink technology to provide 130 TB/s of low-latency GPU-to-GPU communication across the rack, interconnecting all nodes and delivering the performance of 'one big GPU.'
The GB200 NVL72 compute tray uses fifth-generation NVLink with 1.8 TB/s GPU-to-GPU interconnect bandwidth per switch tray, with 9 NVLink Switch Trays each having 144 NVLink ports.
The GB200 NVL72 compute tray's networking uses ConnectX-7 NICs at 400 Gb/s for scale-out, while NVLink provides the high-bandwidth intra-rack GPU-to-GPU communication.
The GTC 2025 roadmap confirms the GB200 NVL72 uses 5th-generation NVLink at 1800 GB/s scale-up bandwidth and is liquid-cooled.

The NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 platform uses NVLink to interconnect all 72 Blackwell GPUs and 36 Grace CPUs into a rack-scale system, alongside ConnectX-7 NICs for scale-out, and scales out with Quantum-X800 InfiniBand or Spectrum-X Ethernet.

research_gaps.description: No optical transceiver or cabling specifications found.
